The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | Caption = | Alternate name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | Dimensions = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | Burial Facility = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | Construction Era = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | Special Notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | Icon = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label Position = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not designated as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, forming part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesInformation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | Caption = | Alternate name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | Burial Facility = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | Construction Era = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | Special Notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | Icon = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label Position = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not designated as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, forming part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesInformation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not designated as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, forming part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesInformation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | Caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m

(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | Icon = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not designated as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, forming part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesInformation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | Caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | burial = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | Icon = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not designated as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, forming part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesInformation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | Caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | burial = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | Icon = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not designated as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, forming part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesInformation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not designated as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, forming part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesInformation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | Caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | burial = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not designated as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, forming part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesInformation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == Burial Facilities == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays artifacts from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | Caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | burial = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not designation1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, forming part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesInformation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not other_designation1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | Caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | burial = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not other_designation1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not other_designation1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | Caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | burial = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage Designation = None | Cultural Property = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not other_designation1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukando Kofun}} revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
