(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = None | designation1 = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}} ary Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | burial = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = None | designation1 = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}} ary Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = None | designation1 = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}} ary The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}} ary [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:塚堂古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[ja:塚堂古墳]] [[sn:sukadō Kofun]] [[en:Tsukadō Kofun]] {{citation needed|Tsukando Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukadō Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | burial = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = None | designation1 = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ary {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukando Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukadō Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:塚堂古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[ja:塚堂古墳]] [[sn:sukadō Kofun]] [[en:Tsukadō Kofun]]

The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ary {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukando Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukadō Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:塚堂古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[ja:塚堂古墳]] [[sn:sukadō Kofun]] [[en:Tsukadō Kofun]]

It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ary {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukando Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukadō Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:塚堂古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[ja:塚堂古墳]] [[sn:sukadō Kofun]] [[en:Tsukadō Kofun]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:塚堂古墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | burial = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = None | designation1 = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ary {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukando Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukadō Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[ja:塚堂古墳]] [[sn:sukadō Kofun]] [[en:Tsukadō Kofun]]

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:塚堂古墳 up to rev 102541396
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 塚堂古墳 |画像 = |画像キャプション = |別名 = 徳丸古墳 |所属 = 若宮古墳群 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]]吉井町徳丸・宮田 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 20|緯度秒 = 44.65 |経度度 = 130|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 24.90 |ISO = JP-40 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長91m
高さ8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 後円部：[[横穴式石室]]
　　　（内部に組合式石棺）
前方部：横穴式石室 |出土品 = 銅鏡・玉類・武器・甲冑・馬具・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 1墳丘2石室 |地図 = Japan Fukuoka |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 塚堂古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = 15 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|若宮古墳群分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1=[[月岡古墳]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[日岡古墳]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3='''塚堂古墳''' | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''塚堂古墳'''（つかんどうこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]吉井町徳丸・宮田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。若宮古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+若宮古墳群一覧 !古墳名 !colspan=2|墳丘 !埋葬施設 !築造時期 !史跡 !備考 |- |[[月岡古墳]]||前方後円墳||95m||竪穴式石室ほか
（長持形石棺）||5c中||なし||出土品は重要文化財 |- |'''塚堂古墳'''||前方後円墳||91m||横穴式石室2基||5c後半||なし|| |- |[[日岡古墳]]||前方後円墳||74m||横穴式石室||6c初頭||国史跡||装飾古墳 |}
福岡県南部、[[筑後川]]南岸の台地上に築造された古墳である。[[月岡古墳]]・[[日岡古墳]]とともに若宮古墳群を形成する。[[昭和28年西日本水害|昭和28年大水害]]復旧工事の際に後円部墳丘は大きく採土されている。[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）・[[1956年]]（昭和31年）に前方部石室が、[[1955年]]（昭和30年）に後円部石室が発掘調査され、[[1979年]]（昭和54年）以降に墳丘・周濠の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、墳丘主軸を東西方向として、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘外表では河原石による[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]（人物・動物・盾形・靫形・蓋形・家形埴輪）が認められる史跡説明板。。墳丘周囲には二重の盾形の周濠が巡らされている。内濠・内堤・外濠は幅8-10メートル・深さ1.5メートルを測り、濠の斜面には墳丘同様の葺石が認められ、濠内は水をたたえたとみられる。埋葬施設は、後円部・前方部における[[横穴式石室]]各1基である。いずれも古式の横穴式石室であり、後円部石室（1号石室、非現存）は大きく破壊された状態で、前方部石室（2号石室、現存・調査後埋め戻し）は未盗掘の状態でそれぞれ発掘調査がなされ、銅鏡・玉類・武器・甲冑・馬具など多数の副葬品が検出されている。特に、前方部石室の馬具の年代が先代の月岡古墳の時期に近く、先代から受け継いで副葬された可能性がある点、前方部石室からは武器・武具が多い一方で、後円部石室からは装飾具が多く出土している点で特色を示す。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半ごろと推定される。若宮古墳群では月岡古墳に後続し、日岡古墳に先行する時期に位置づけられる。月岡古墳が畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との強いつながりを示すのに比して、畿内色が薄れ在地色が強まりつつある様相を示す古墳である。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）、長野堰水門工事の際に大石を持ち去ったと伝承（後円部石室石材か）{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 * [[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）、家屋新築に伴う前方部石室の発見・調査：第1次（宮崎勇蔵ら、[[1935年|1935]]・[[1938年]]に「徳丸古墳」・「俗称塚堂古墳」として報告）{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 * [[1955年]]（昭和30年）、[[昭和28年西日本水害|昭和28年大水害]]復旧工事の後円部墳丘採土に伴う後円部石室の応急調査：第2次（[[福岡県立浮羽究真館高等学校|浮羽高校]]考古学部、[[1983年]]報告に収録）{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 * [[1956年]]（昭和31年）、前方部石室内・閉塞石前面の調査：第3次（浮羽高校考古学部、1983年報告に収録）。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）、県営圃場整備事業に伴う墳丘北・東側外周の発掘調査：第4次（福岡県教育委員会、[[1982年]]に報告）。 * 1979年（昭和54年）、[[国道210号]]浮羽バイパス建設工事に伴う周濠部の発掘調査：第5次（福岡県教育委員会、1983年に報告）{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）、県営圃場整備事業に伴う墳丘西側外周の発掘調査：第6次（福岡県教育委員会、1983年に報告）{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約91メートル筑後川流域装飾古墳同時公開解説シート。（残存70メートル） * 後円部 ** 直径：約64メートル ** 高さ：約8メートル史跡説明板。 * 前方部 ** 幅：推定約68メートル ** 高さ：約7.5メートル == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、後円部・前方部のそれぞれにおいて[[横穴式石室]]が構築されている。いずれも古式の石室である。 '''後円部石室（1号石室）'''は、大きく破壊された状態で発見されている（非現存）。前方部石室よりも一回り大きい規模で、南西方向（くびれ部方向）に開口したとみられる。内部に組合式石棺を据えており、石棺片が出土しているほか、多数の副葬品が検出されている。 '''前方部石室（2号石室）'''は、発見時は未盗掘のほぼ完存状態であった（現在は埋め戻し）。単室構造の石室で、西方向に開口し、玄室は長さ約3.1メートル・幅約1.6メートル・高さ約1.9メートルを測る。割石の小口積みによって構築され、壁面には赤色顔料が塗布される。奥壁と両側壁には刀・鉾を載せる突起があったという（非現存）。石室内では、首飾りをして貝製の腕輪を右手にはめた壮年男性の人骨とともに、多数の副葬品が検出されている。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:塚堂古墳出土 鋲留短甲.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|2号石室 短甲}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:塚堂古墳 2号石室 2号短甲.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|2号石室 短甲}}{{small|吉井歴史民俗資料館展示。}}]] [[ファイル:塚堂古墳 1号石室 三環鈴.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|1号石室 三環鈴}}{{small|吉井歴史民俗資料館展示。}}]] 塚堂古墳の石室内で検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" !rowspan=2| !rowspan=2|後円部石室（1号石室）
（1955年） !colspan=2|前方部石室（2号石室）

土師器片 |砥石 1 | |} 後円部石室では装飾具が多く出土したのに対して、前方部石室では武器・武具が多く出土した点で特色を示す吉井歴史民俗資料館展示解説。。また、前方部出土の馬具は月岡古墳のものと年代が近く、先代から受け継いで副葬した可能性が指摘される。 塚堂古墳 出土品-2.JPG|装身具
{{small|吉井歴史民俗資料館展示（他画像も同様）。}} 塚堂古墳 出土品-4.JPG|鉄鏃・馬具 塚堂古墳 出土品-1.JPG|鉄鉾・武具・農工具 塚堂古墳 出土品-3.JPG|鉄刀・鉄剣 塚堂古墳 形象埴輪.JPG|形象埴輪 塚堂古墳 盾形埴輪・円筒埴輪.JPG|盾形埴輪・円筒埴輪 == 関連施設 == * 吉井歴史民俗資料館（うきは市吉井町） - 塚堂古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 * [[東京国立博物館]]（東京都台東区） - 塚堂古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（うきは市教育委員会、2007年設置） * 筑後川流域装飾古墳同時公開解説シート * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=塚堂古墳の調査|url=http://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.57503 |title=塚堂遺跡I -福岡県浮羽郡吉井町所在遺跡の調査-|series=一般国道210号線浮羽バイパス関係埋蔵文化財調査報告第1集|publisher=福岡県教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[大塚初重]]|year=1989|chapter=塚堂古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-10260-7|ref={{Harvid|塚堂古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=若宮古墳群|title=福岡県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]41|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4-582-49041-7|ref={{Harvid|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=田中幸夫|title=筑後千年村徳丸古墳前方部石室に於ける埋葬の状と遺物の一二|date=1935-01-15|journal=考古學雜誌|publisher=考古學會|volume=25|number=1|pages=037-042|url=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1938|chapter=筑後国浮羽郡千年村徳丸塚堂古墳|title=史蹟名勝天然紀念物調査報告書|volume=第10輯|publisher=福岡県|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1977|chapter=|title=吉井町誌|volume=第1巻|publisher=吉井町|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=|title=塚堂古墳 -福岡県浮羽郡吉井町宮田所在古墳の調査-|series=吉井町文化財調査報告書第1集|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=若宮古墳群1 -月岡古墳・塚堂古墳・日岡古墳-|series=吉井町文化財調査報告書第4集|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=|title=若宮古墳群2 -塚堂古墳・日岡古墳-|series=吉井町文化財調査報告書第6集|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * 若宮古墳群 ** [[月岡古墳]] ** [[日岡古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html 若宮古墳群（日岡・月岡・塚堂古墳）] - うきは市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:つかんとうこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:うきは市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 塚堂古墳 |画像 = |画像キャプション = |別名 = 徳丸古墳 |所属 = 若宮古墳群 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]]吉井町徳丸・宮田 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 20|緯度秒 = 44.65 |経度度 = 130|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 24.90 |ISO = JP-40 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長91m
高さ8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 後円部：[[横穴式石室]]
　　　（内部に組合式石棺）
前方部：横穴式石室 |出土品 = 銅鏡・玉類・武器・甲冑・馬具・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 1墳丘2石室 |地図 = Japan Fukuoka |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 塚堂古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = 15 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|若宮古墳群分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1=[[月岡古墳]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[日岡古墳]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3='''塚堂古墳''' | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''塚堂古墳'''（つかんどうこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]吉井町徳丸・宮田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。若宮古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+若宮古墳群一覧 !古墳名 !colspan=2|墳丘 !埋葬施設 !築造時期 !史跡 !備考 |- |[[月岡古墳]]||前方後円墳||95m||竪穴式石室ほか
（長持形石棺）||5c中||なし||出土品は重要文化財 |- |'''塚堂古墳'''||前方後円墳||91m||横穴式石室2基||5c後半||なし|| |- |[[日岡古墳]]||前方後円墳||74m||横穴式石室||6c初頭||国史跡||装飾古墳 |}
福岡県南部、[[筑後川]]南岸の台地上に築造された古墳である。[[月岡古墳]]・[[日岡古墳]]とともに若宮古墳群を形成する。[[昭和28年西日本水害|昭和28年大水害]]復旧工事の際に後円部墳丘は大きく採土されている。[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）・[[1956年]]（昭和31年）に前方部石室が、[[1955年]]（昭和30年）に後円部石室が発掘調査され、[[1979年]]（昭和54年）以降に墳丘・周濠の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、墳丘主軸を東西方向として、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘外表では河原石による[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]（人物・動物・盾形・靫形・蓋形・家形埴輪）が認められる史跡説明板。。墳丘周囲には二重の盾形の周濠が巡らされている。内濠・内堤・外濠は幅8-10メートル・深さ1.5メートルを測り、濠の斜面には墳丘同様の葺石が認められ、濠内は水をたたえたとみられる。埋葬施設は、後円部・前方部における[[横穴式石室]]各1基である。いずれも古式の横穴式石室であり、後円部石室（1号石室、非現存）は大きく破壊された状態で、前方部石室（2号石室、現存・調査後埋め戻し）は未盗掘の状態でそれぞれ発掘調査がなされ、銅鏡・玉類・武器・甲冑・馬具など多数の副葬品が検出されている。特に、前方部石室の馬具の年代が先代の月岡古墳の時期に近く、先代から受け継いで副葬された可能性がある点、前方部石室からは武器・武具が多い一方で、後円部石室からは装飾具が多く出土している点で特色を示す。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半ごろと推定される。若宮古墳群では月岡古墳に後続し、日岡古墳に先行する時期に位置づけられる。月岡古墳が畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との強いつながりを示すのに比して、畿内色が薄れ在地色が強まりつつある様相を示す古墳である。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）、長野堰水門工事の際に大石を持ち去ったと伝承（後円部石室石材か）{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 * [[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）、家屋新築に伴う前方部石室の発見・調査：第1次（宮崎勇蔵ら、[[1935年|1935]]・[[1938年]]に「徳丸古墳」・「俗称塚堂古墳」として報告）{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 * [[1955年]]（昭和30年）、[[昭和28年西日本水害|昭和28年大水害]]復旧工事の後円部墳丘採土に伴う後円部石室の応急調査：第2次（[[福岡県立浮羽究真館高等学校|浮羽高校]]考古学部、[[1983年]]報告に収録）{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 * [[1956年]]（昭和31年）、前方部石室内・閉塞石前面の調査：第3次（浮羽高校考古学部、1983年報告に収録）。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）、県営圃場整備事業に伴う墳丘北・東側外周の発掘調査：第4次（福岡県教育委員会、[[1982年]]に報告）。 * 1979年（昭和54年）、[[国道210号]]浮羽バイパス建設工事に伴う周濠部の発掘調査：第5次（福岡県教育委員会、1983年に報告）{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）、県営圃場整備事業に伴う墳丘西側外周の発掘調査：第6次（福岡県教育委員会、1983年に報告）{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約91メートル筑後川流域装飾古墳同時公開解説シート。（残存70メートル） * 後円部 ** 直径：約64メートル ** 高さ：約8メートル史跡説明板。 * 前方部 ** 幅：推定約68メートル ** 高さ：約7.5メートル == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、後円部・前方部のそれぞれにおいて[[横穴式石室]]が構築されている。いずれも古式の石室である。 '''後円部石室（1号石室）'''は、大きく破壊された状態で発見されている（非現存）。前方部石室よりも一回り大きい規模で、南西方向（くびれ部方向）に開口したとみられる。内部に組合式石棺を据えており、石棺片が出土しているほか、多数の副葬品が検出されている。 '''前方部石室（2号石室）'''は、発見時は未盗掘のほぼ完存状態であった（現在は埋め戻し）。単室構造の石室で、西方向に開口し、玄室は長さ約3.1メートル・幅約1.6メートル・高さ約1.9メートルを測る。割石の小口積みによって構築され、壁面には赤色顔料が塗布される。奥壁と両側壁には刀・鉾を載せる突起があったという（非現存）。石室内では、首飾りをして貝製の腕輪を右手にはめた壮年男性の人骨とともに、多数の副葬品が検出されている。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:塚堂古墳出土 鋲留短甲.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|2号石室 短甲}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:塚堂古墳 2号石室 2号短甲.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|2号石室 短甲}}{{small|吉井歴史民俗資料館展示。}}]] [[ファイル:塚堂古墳 1号石室 三環鈴.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|1号石室 三環鈴}}{{small|吉井歴史民俗資料館展示。}}]] 塚堂古墳の石室内で検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" !rowspan=2| !rowspan=2|後円部石室（1号石室）
（1955年） !colspan=2|前方部石室（2号石室）

土師器片 |砥石 1 | |} 後円部石室では装飾具が多く出土したのに対して、前方部石室では武器・武具が多く出土した点で特色を示す吉井歴史民俗資料館展示解説。。また、前方部出土の馬具は月岡古墳のものと年代が近く、先代から受け継いで副葬した可能性が指摘される。 塚堂古墳 出土品-2.JPG|装身具
{{small|吉井歴史民俗資料館展示（他画像も同様）。}} 塚堂古墳 出土品-4.JPG|鉄鏃・馬具 塚堂古墳 出土品-1.JPG|鉄鉾・武具・農工具 塚堂古墳 出土品-3.JPG|鉄刀・鉄剣 塚堂古墳 形象埴輪.JPG|形象埴輪 塚堂古墳 盾形埴輪・円筒埴輪.JPG|盾形埴輪・円筒埴輪 == 関連施設 == * 吉井歴史民俗資料館（うきは市吉井町） - 塚堂古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 * [[東京国立博物館]]（東京都台東区） - 塚堂古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（うきは市教育委員会、2007年設置） * 筑後川流域装飾古墳同時公開解説シート * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=塚堂古墳の調査|url=http://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.57503 |title=塚堂遺跡I -福岡県浮羽郡吉井町所在遺跡の調査-|series=一般国道210号線浮羽バイパス関係埋蔵文化財調査報告第1集|publisher=福岡県教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|塚堂遺跡I|1983}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[大塚初重]]|year=1989|chapter=塚堂古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-10260-7|ref={{Harvid|塚堂古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=若宮古墳群|title=福岡県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]41|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4-582-49041-7|ref={{Harvid|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=田中幸夫|title=筑後千年村徳丸古墳前方部石室に於ける埋葬の状と遺物の一二|date=1935-01-15|journal=考古學雜誌|publisher=考古學會|volume=25|number=1|pages=037-042|url=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1938|chapter=筑後国浮羽郡千年村徳丸塚堂古墳|title=史蹟名勝天然紀念物調査報告書|volume=第10輯|publisher=福岡県|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1977|chapter=|title=吉井町誌|volume=第1巻|publisher=吉井町|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=|title=塚堂古墳 -福岡県浮羽郡吉井町宮田所在古墳の調査-|series=吉井町文化財調査報告書第1集|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=若宮古墳群1 -月岡古墳・塚堂古墳・日岡古墳-|series=吉井町文化財調査報告書第4集|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=|title=若宮古墳群2 -塚堂古墳・日岡古墳-|series=吉井町文化財調査報告書第6集|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * 若宮古墳群 ** [[月岡古墳]] ** [[日岡古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html 若宮古墳群（日岡・月岡・塚堂古墳）] - うきは市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:つかんとうこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:うきは市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = None | designation1 = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ary {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukando Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukadō Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[ja:塚堂古墳]] [[sn:sukadō Kofun]] [[en:Tsukadō Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|塚堂古墳|version=102541396|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = None | designation1 = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ary {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚堂古墳|version=102541396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[ja:塚堂古墳]] [[sn:sukadō Kofun]] [[en:Tsukadō Kofun]]

Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = None | designation1 = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Kofun|qq=}} ary {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚堂古墳|version=102541396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[ja:塚堂古墳]] [[sn:sukadō Kofun]] [[en:Tsukadō Kofun]]

Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hinooka Kofun|Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[:en:Hinooka Kofun|Hioka Kofun]] ary {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚堂古墳|version=102541396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[ja:塚堂古墳]] [[sn:sukadō Kofun]] [[en:Tsukadō Kofun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsukandō Kofun | image = | caption = | alternate_name = Tokumaru Kofun | Cluster = Wakamiya Kofun Group | location = Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tomb (前方後円墳) | area = Mound length: 91 m
Height: 8 m (rear circular mound) | burial = Rear mound: horizontal stone chamber
(with composite stone coffin)
Front mound: horizontal stone chamber | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jewelry, weapons, armor, horse gear, haniwa | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 5th century | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = None | designation1 = | notes = One mound with two stone chambers | map = Japan Fukuoka | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Tsukandō Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Wakamiya Kofun Group}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | map_dot_label1=[[Tsukioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos1=top | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | map_dot_label2=[[Hinooka Kofun|Hioka Kofun]] | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | map_dot_label3='''Tsukandō Kofun''' | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''Tsukandō Kofun''' (塚堂古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in Tokumaru/Miyata, Yoshii-machi, Ukiha City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The tomb is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and is characterized as a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳). It is not Historic Site1 as a heritage site. == Overview == Tsukandō Kofun is situated on a plateau south of the Chikugo River, typeing part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group along with [[Tsukioka Kofun]] and [[Hinooka Kofun|Hioka Kofun]]. The mound, oriented along an east-west axis, features a rear circular mound and a front rectangular mound. The tomb's external surface includes paving stones (葺石), cylindrical haniwa, and various shaped haniwa such as figures, animals, shields, quivers, lids, and housesIntypeation board at the site.. Surrounding the mound are double moats, which are shield-shaped in plan and likely held water, with paving stones lining the moat slopes. The mound houses two horizontal stone chambers: one in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. Both chambers are ancient in design. The rear chamber (1st chamber) has been destroyed, while the front chamber (2nd chamber) remains intact but has been reburied after excavation. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, beads, weapons, armor, and horse gear, were discovered in these chambers. The mound is dated to the late 5th century, falling within the Kofun period. It represents a transitional phase where local styles began to dominate over influences from the central Yamato polity. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the tomb include: * '''Adornment''': Bronze mirrors, glass and stone beads, jade, and gilt-bronze ornaments. * '''Weapons''': Swords, spears, and iron arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Helmets, cuirasses, and other protective gear. * '''Horse Gear''': Bits, stirrups, ornamental fittings, and bells. * '''Haniwa''': Cylindrical and variously shaped. The distribution of grave goods suggests a division of roles, with the rear chamber associated with adornment items and the front chamber emphasizing weapons and tools. Notably, some horse gear appears to have been inherited from earlier burials in the group, such as the [[Tsukioka Kofun]]Exhibit explanation, Yoshii Historical Folk Museum.. == burial == The rear chamber (1st chamber) is larger and heavily damaged, likely due to earlier disturbances. It contained a composite stone coffin and multiple grave goods. The front chamber (2nd chamber) was intact upon discovery, featuring a single-room structure with red pigment applied to the walls and weapon mounts embedded in the stone. Measurements of the front chamber: * Length: ~3.1 m * Width: ~1.6 m * Height: ~1.9 m == Related Facilities == * '''Yoshii Historical Folk Museum''': Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukandō Kofun. * '''Tokyo National Museum''': Also holds select grave goods from the site. == Access == * By Car: Located near National Route 210 and accessible via local roads. * Public Transport: Accessible from nearby bus stops with a short walk. == See Also == * [[Wakamiya Kofun Group]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[Hinooka Kofun|Hioka Kofun]] * [[Tsukioka Kofun]] * [[:en:Hinooka Kofun|Hioka Kofun]] ary {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚堂古墳|version=102541396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]][[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[ja:塚堂古墳]] [[sn:sukadō Kofun]] [[en:Tsukadō Kofun]] [[Category:うきは市の歴史]]
